assertion_results.text: The response describes locating the digest comment using the marker string '[sdlc-workflow] Description digest:' from shared/description-digest-protocol.md
assertion_results.evidence: In digest-mismatch.md, Section 2 ('Locate the Digest Comment') explicitly states: 'Search all returned comments for those whose body starts with the marker string: [sdlc-workflow] Description digest:' (lines 22-26). The summary table also reiterates: 'Search all comments for marker prefix `[sdlc-workflow] Description digest:`' (line 127).

assertion_results.text: The response describes detecting a mismatch between the stored digest and the computed SHA-256 hash of the current description
assertion_results.evidence: In digest-mismatch.md, Section 4 extracts the stored digest ('sha256:0000...', line 49), Section 5 describes computing SHA-256 of the current description with proper normalization (lines 54-67), and Section 6 ('Compare Digests -- Mismatch Detected') explicitly states: 'The stored digest and the computed digest do not match' (line 77), showing both expected and actual values (lines 80-81).

assertion_results.text: The response alerts the user that the description was modified after plan-feature created the task and displays the expected vs actual digest values
assertion_results.evidence: In digest-mismatch.md, Section 7 ('Alert the User') presents a message stating: 'The task description for TC-9201 has been modified since plan-feature created it.' (line 92) and displays both values: 'Expected digest (from plan-feature comment): sha256:0000...' and 'Actual digest (computed from current description): sha256:7f83b1...' (lines 94-95).

assertion_results.text: The response offers the user a choice: (1) proceed with the current description, or (2) stop so they can re-run plan-feature
assertion_results.evidence: In digest-mismatch.md, Section 7 presents two choices: '1. Proceed -- accept the current description as-is and continue with implementation' and '2. Stop -- halt execution so you can re-run plan-feature to regenerate tasks with the updated description' (lines 100-101), followed by 'Choose (1/2):' (line 103).

assertion_results.text: The response stops execution and does not proceed to Step 2 or implementation planning until the user responds — following the same pause-and-ask pattern as the incomplete description handling
assertion_results.evidence: In digest-mismatch.md, Section 8 ('Stop Execution Immediately') explicitly states: 'execution stops immediately after presenting the choice to the user. No subsequent steps are performed until the user responds' (line 107). It lists each step that is NOT entered: 'Step 2 (Verify Dependencies) is NOT entered. Step 3 (Transition to In Progress) is NOT entered. Step 4 (Understand the Code) is NOT entered. Step 5 (Create Branch) is NOT entered.' (lines 109-112) and concludes: 'The skill is fully blocked, waiting for the user's explicit decision.' (line 115).
